questions.question: What is the main advantage of index investing over stock picking?
options: Lower cost and better long-term performance
questions.explanation: Over 90% of active fund managers underperform index funds over 15+ years, largely due to higher fees. Index funds offer broad diversification at minimal cost.

questions.question: What is an expense ratio?
options: The cost to open a brokerage account
The annual fee charged by a fund as a % of assets
The tax rate on fund gains
questions.explanation: The expense ratio is the annual fee a fund charges, expressed as a percentage of your investment. Lower is better — 0.03% vs 1.00% saves thousands over decades.

questions.question: How does an ETF differ from a mutual fund?
options: ETFs trade on exchanges in real-time; mutual funds trade at end-of-day NAV
Mutual funds always have lower fees
questions.explanation: ETFs trade throughout the day on exchanges like stocks, while mutual fund shares are bought/sold at the end-of-day NAV price.

questions.question: What is the 'three-fund portfolio'?
options: US stocks + international stocks + bonds (using index funds)
questions.explanation: The three-fund portfolio consists of a US stock index fund, an international stock index fund, and a bond index fund — simple, diversified, and low-cost.
